title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the ideal banking angle, we need to consider the forces acting on a vehicle moving in a circular path. The centripetal force required for the turn is provided by the horizontal component of the normal force.

title: Centripetal Force Equation
text: The centripetal force \(F_c\) needed to keep a vehicle moving in a circle of radius \(r\) at speed \(v\) is given by the equation \(F_c=m*v**2/r\), where \(m\) is the mass of the vehicle.

title: Banking Angle Equation
text: The ideal banking angle \(\theta\) can be found using the equation \(tan(\theta)=v**2/(r*g)\), where \(g\) is the acceleration due to gravity.

title: Calculate the Banking Angle
text: Given $$\(v=105\)$$ $$\frac{km}{h}$$ (convert to m/s), $$\(r=1200\)$$ $$m$$, and $$\(g=9.8\)$$ m/s², calculate \(tan(\theta)\) and then find \(\theta\).
